OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
I'm a curious guy who's lived through the experience of being an immigrant, so I've seen firsthand how misinformation can spread like wildfire. As someone who spends just an hour a day scrolling through these platforms – maybe TikTok or Facebook, but not too much – I think dealing with fake content is about staying grounded.

My dad has that PhD and always tells me to double-check things because he remembers the importance of education from back home in Pakistan. Even though my own schooling only went up to lower secondary here in Italy where Bari is located, I make it a point to fact-check whenever something pops up on social media that sounds too good or too scary.

Being a mechanic taught me practical skills and problem-solving – you don't just hope around an engine issue; you diagnose properly. That same logic applies online: if content doesn't add up with my knowledge, I question it more than others might assume given my moderate agreeableness score.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary:
I derived this perspective by considering how my background as an immigrant shapes my view on misinformation (having experienced cultural shifts where accuracy matters). My lower secondary education plus my father's PhD influenced my approach to verification. The mechanical trade instills practical thinking which translates into evaluating online claims.

The high neuroticism might make me more sensitive than average when encountering questionable content, but that curiosity helps drive the need for scrutiny.
